title: Domain newtype nits: timestamps, Assignee, group_labels
description:
Resolve the low-severity domain newtype nits (report nits). In scope despite low severity ‚Äî the quality bar is high and we are not deferring these.

## Sites
- `domain/types.rs:205-207` ‚Äî `Issue.created_at`/`updated_at` are raw RFC-3339 `String` while the sibling `GateState.updated_at` and all `Event`/`GateRunResult` timestamps use `DateTime<Utc>`. Change both to `DateTime<Utc>` (chrono's default serde emits RFC 3339); the constructors already build a `DateTime`. Update the storage assignment sites (`storage/json.rs`, `storage/memory.rs`) to assign the `DateTime` directly.
- `domain/types.rs:163-168,184` ‚Äî `Issue.assignee`/`GateState.updated_by` are `Option<String>` holding the documented `{type}:{identifier}` format, validated inconsistently (`bulk_update.rs:530` checks; `issue.rs:554`/`claim.rs:99` don't). Introduce an `Assignee { kind: String, identifier: String }` newtype with `FromStr` (typed error) + `Display` (renders `kind:identifier`); use `Option<Assignee>` and centralize the duplicated colon-split (`labels.rs:185` validate_assignee_format, `agent_config.rs:196`). Event fields using the same format follow suit.
- `domain/projection.rs:150-160` (`group_labels`) ‚Äî inline `split_once(':')` duplicates `crate::labels::parse_label` (already re-exported into `domain/`); fold over `parse_label(label).ok()` instead.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `Issue.created_at`/`updated_at` are `DateTime<Utc>`; no RFC-3339 `String` timestamp field remains on `Issue`, and serialized JSON stays RFC-3339.
- [ ] An `Assignee` newtype with `FromStr`/`Display` represents the `{type}:{identifier}` format; `Issue.assignee` and `GateState.updated_by` use `Option<Assignee>`, and the assignee format is validated through one path (no caller sets a raw unvalidated string).
- [ ] `group_labels` parses labels via the shared `parse_label`, with no inline `split_once(':')`.
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` pass; existing issue JSON files continue to deserialize (single-consumer repo ‚Äî no broad migration required, but the project's own `.jit/` data must still load).
